title: Routing
learning_objectives: Understand the purpose of Routing in SAP PP.
Learn how manufacturing operations are defined.
Understand Routing components and important fields.
Learn standard SAP Routing transaction codes.
Understand how Routing integrates with Work Centers and Production Orders.

introduction.title: What is Routing?
introduction.content: Routing is a master data object in SAP Production Planning that defines the sequence of operations required to manufacture a product. It specifies the Work Centers where operations are performed, the processing time for each operation and the resources required during manufacturing.

business_purpose.title: Why is Routing Important?
business_purpose.content: Routing provides SAP with detailed manufacturing instructions. It enables accurate production scheduling, capacity planning, production costing and operation sequencing. Every Production Order refers to a Routing to determine how a product should be manufactured.

routing_components.description: Contains general information about the Routing.
Machine or resource where each operation is performed.
Defines how an operation is processed.
Stores setup time, machine time and labor time.

integration.purpose: Every Routing operation is assigned to a Work Center.
Routing is linked with BOM through Production Version.
Operations are copied into Production Orders.

business_example.title: Example
business_example.content: During the production of a stainless steel sheet, Routing defines operations such as Cutting, Hot Rolling, Pickling, Annealing, Cold Rolling, Grinding and Final Inspection. Each operation is assigned to its respective Work Center with predefined processing times.

best_practices: Maintain operations in the correct sequence.
Assign the correct Work Center to every operation.
common_errors: Routing not linked with Production Version.
important_notes: A Routing can contain multiple operations.
Operations are executed in the defined sequence.
Routing directly affects scheduling, costing and production execution.
